meta.enso_source: COBE-SST2 + GPCP observations - Niño 3.4 standardized index
meta.thresholds: OBS event catalog (same classification as Map Explorer)
caveats: Pre-2000 data is subject to reporting biases (EM-DAT documentation). Event counts grow substantially after 2000 due to improved reporting.
Only 25-50% of events have economic damage estimates depending on decade; deaths and affected are more complete (67-73%).
Current year data (2025-2026) is provisional - validated at start of subsequent year.
Phase assignment uses the ENSO phase at each event's start month. Events missing a start month are assigned the year's dominant phase.

Death and damage ratios can be heavily influenced by a small number of catastrophic events (e.g. 1982-83 El Niño droughts in Ethiopia/Sudan account for ~450k deaths in El Niño years). Interpret medians alongside means.
This is a descriptive correlation analysis - ENSO is one of many drivers of disaster losses. No causal attribution is implied.
Regional patterns vary substantially. A global aggregate may mask opposing signals in different regions - see the Map Explorer for spatial detail.

GBR.name: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

HKG.name: China, Hong Kong Special Administrative Region

intensity_caveat: Intensity phase uses the same 7-class OBS event catalog as the Map Explorer (COBE-SST2 + GPCP, with event duration ≥5 months, DJF phase-locking, and gap-filling applied). Each EM-DAT event is assigned the ENSO phase of its start month. Months not covered by any detected ENSO event are labeled neutral.
